decisions.decision: Inverted-index candidate pruning: build neighbor-to-symbols map, generate candidate pairs from co-occurrence, compute Jaccard only for candidates
decisions.rationale: Most symbol pairs share zero neighbors. Current O(n^2) loop wastes time on pairs that can never produce a result. Inverted index reduces to O(E + C) where C << n^2.

decisions.decision: compute_coupling() signature and CouplingResult dataclass remain unchanged
decisions.rationale: Purely internal optimization. No callers need to change.

decisions.decision: Must produce identical results to the current O(n^2) implementation for all inputs
decisions.rationale: This is a performance refactor, not a behavior change. Existing test suite serves as regression gate.

decisions.decision: Build inverted index from the same edge data already fetched by _build_neighbor_sets; reuse neighbor_sets dict for Jaccard computation
decisions.rationale: No additional storage queries needed. Single pass over edges builds both neighbor_sets and inverted index.

decisions.decision: No threshold-based early-exit optimization (e.g., max possible Jaccard upper bound pruning)
decisions.rationale: Adds algorithmic complexity for marginal gain. The inverted-index pruning alone eliminates the dominant cost (pairs with zero intersection).

constraints: Python stdlib only — no numpy, scipy, or external deps
Existing test suite (test_context_coupling.py, 14 tests) must pass unchanged
CouplingResult ordering (sorted by -strength) must be preserved
COUPLED_WITH relationship creation must remain identical
